items.title: Rescuers search through rubble of collapsed buildings after earthquake in Myanmar
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Myanmar’s ruling military said Saturday on state television that the confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644.
Collapsed buildings could be seen in Mandalay, the country's No. 2 city, as rescue workers and volunteers tried to recover any possible survivors under rubble. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Hundreds of thousands rally in Istanbul in support of imprisoned mayor
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Turkey's main opposition party, the Republican People's Party (CHP) organized the demonstration, the latest in a series of protests that resulted in hundreds of detentions and have turned up the pressure on the country's long-time leader, President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Fatalities and many injured after Russian drone strike on Ukraine's Dnipro
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Dnipropetrovsk Regional Military Administration said the strikes damaged a hotel and restaurant complex, dozens of apartment and residential buildings, an administrative building, and an educational institution. 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Video shows power of blast from strike in central Gaza, as Israel continues its offensive in enclave
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The blast from a strike was caught on camera on Saturday in Deir al-Balah, central Gaza, as Israel continues its offensive in the enclave.
A big crater was left at the site, not far from Al Aqsa Martyrs hospital. There were no immediate reports of casualties but some nearby buildings were damaged. 
(AP video shot by: Wafaa Shurafa,  Abdel Kareem Hana)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Skygazers across Europe enthralled by partial solar eclipse as moon takes bite out of sun
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The eclipse was visible Saturday across Europe, western Africa, eastern North America and northern Asia. During a partial solar eclipse, the moon passes between the sun and Earth. The moon casts a shadow on Earth and only partly blocks the sun, making it appear like a crescent. 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Rescuers search for survivors in Myanmar's earthquake-ravaged capital
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Dozens of patients were seen in beds outside a damaged hospital in Naypyitaw on Saturday as the city struggled to cope with the destruction from a powerful earthquake that rocked the country, killing more than 1,000 people.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Republican Rep. Victoria Spartz booed at a town hall in Indiana
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>As with other town halls held by Republicans across the country, Congresswoman Victoria Spartz was met in her Indiana district by a group of angry constituents who challenged her on the decisions being made by the Trump administration, from threatening to cut public media funding to deporting migrants without due process.  

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Satellite photos show control tower at Naypyitaw International Airport collapsed in quake
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Friday's devastating earthquake that struck Myanmar caused the air traffic control tower at Naypyitaw International Airport to collapse. Satellite images taken by Planet Labs PBC and analyzed by The Associated Press show the tower toppled over as if sheered from its base.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Black women’s hair products are in the safety spotlight. Here’s what to know
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Black women around the world gather in salons, dorm rooms and living rooms for hours at a time to get synthetic braids put in their hair. But they're wondering if the convenience and fashion benefits are being outweighed by potential health hazards.

#hairproducts #health</article>

items.title: India sends air force plane carrying aid and rescue team lands to Yangon
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The aid had been loaded onto the plane in Ghaziabad, with the External Affairs Ministry saying it was the "first tranche of 15 tonnes of relief material" included tents, blankets, sleeping bags, food packets, hygiene kits, generators, and medicines.

items.title: Time running out for workers trapped under collapsed Bangkok building; AP explains
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Workers in Bangkok were scrambling to clear rubble and rescue any survivors from a powerful earthquake that hit Thailand and neighbouring Myanmar on Friday. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Opponents and supporters of South Korea's impeached president rally in Seoul
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A large group of protesters gathered in central Seoul on Saturday to urge the Constitutional Court to dismiss South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol. The court has been deliberating on whether to formally remove Yoon from office or reinstate him since the parliament voted to impeach Yoon over his imposition of martial law in December.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Danish foreign minister scolds Trump administration for its criticism of Denmark and Greenland
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Foreign Minister Lars Løkke Rasmussen, make the remarks in a video posted to social media after U.S. Vice President JD Vance’s visit to the strategic island.

items.title: US-Japan relations take centre stage at 80th anniversary of Battle of Iwo Jima
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth praised the strong friendship between U.S. and Japan at the 80th anniversary of Battle of Iwo Jima. Japan’s Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba meanwhile praised the "noble sacrifices" of those who died in the war.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Thai minister hopeful, but time running out for people buried under Bangkok high rise rubble
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thai Deputy Prime Minister Auntin Charnvirakul said Saturday there was still hope to find survivors beneath tons of rubble at a Bangkok building site but that that hope would not extend beyond 72 hours. (AP video/Jerry Harmer, Sompong Saisomboon, produced by Annika Wolters)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
